assertions.expectation: Explains the difference between Rapid and Stable channels — including that Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability while Stable carries a full SLA, and clearly distinguishes minor version vs patch version differences between the channels
assertions.reason: Response mentions Stable is 2-3 versions behind but doesn't explain SLA differences or distinguish between minor vs patch versions clearly

assertions.expectation: Notes that channel changes don't trigger an immediate upgrade or downgrade
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states that changing channels requires creating a new cluster and mentions downgrade isn't supported, missing that channel changes don't trigger immediate version changes

assertions.expectation: Warns that if 1.30 isn't yet available on Stable, the cluster may not receive patches until Stable catches up
assertions.reason: No mention of potential patching delays when switching channels if current version isn't available on target channel

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Only shows command to create new cluster, doesn't provide the gcloud container clusters update command to change release channel

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking version availability on Stable before switching (gcloud container get-server-config)
assertions.reason: No mention of checking version availability on target channel before switching

assertions.expectation: Discusses the impact on auto-upgrade timing going forward
assertions.reason: No discussion of how auto-upgrade timing will change after switching to Stable channel

assertions.expectation: Mentions that the Extended channel is an option if they need even slower cadence (with cost implications)
assertions.reason: Extended channel option is not mentioned as an alternative

assertions.expectation: Advises coordinating the change during a maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response mentions planning during low-traffic period and maintenance window with stakeholders
